claims.evidence_text: The obtained Kc values for wheat were: 0.43–0.68 (Kc ini), 0.75–1.02 (Kc dev), 0.94–1.11 (Kc mid), and 0.4–0.64 (Kc end)
claims.claim_summary: Measured initial-stage crop coefficient (Kc ini) for wheat is 0.43-0.68 in North Nile Delta.

claims.evidence_text: The obtained Kc values for wheat were: 0.43–0.68 (Kc ini), 0.75–1.02 (Kc dev), 0.94–1.11 (Kc mid), and 0.4–0.64 (Kc end)
claims.claim_summary: Measured development-stage crop coefficient (Kc dev) for wheat is 0.75-1.02.

claims.evidence_text: The obtained Kc values for wheat were: 0.43–0.68 (Kc ini), 0.75–1.02 (Kc dev), 0.94–1.11 (Kc mid), and 0.4–0.64 (Kc end)
claims.claim_summary: Measured mid-season crop coefficient (Kc mid) for wheat is 0.94-1.11.

claims.evidence_text: The obtained Kc values for wheat were: 0.43–0.68 (Kc ini), 0.75–1.02 (Kc dev), 0.94–1.11 (Kc mid), and 0.4–0.64 (Kc end)
claims.claim_summary: Measured late-season crop coefficient (Kc end) for wheat is 0.4-0.64.

claims.evidence_text: Accurate crop coefficient (Kc) is essential for optimizing irrigation water use as well as enlargement water productivity in agriculture... local calibration of Kc is necessary for precise irrigation scheduling
claims.claim_summary: Accurate, locally calibrated Kc is essential to optimize irrigation water use and water productivity for wheat.
